raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP-RULES-HEALTH-MONITORING",
      "title": "Missing organ health monitoring and heartbeat SLA rules",
      "description": "The MSP contains no rules defining acceptable heartbeat intervals, missed-pong thresholds, recovery time objectives, or permissible degraded-state duration. Spine-transitions show a repeating cycle of all organs going DEGRADED on a single missed_pong and recovering ~34 seconds later, but there is no operational specification to constrain this behavior or trigger escalation. Without such rules, Nomos cannot enforce reliability or differentiate between transient and systemic failures.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Rules' — currently (none). MSP Purpose requires translation of BoR into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, and constraints.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "critical"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP-GENOME-RISK-TIERS",
      "title": "No organ-specific risk-tier classification or differentiated operating rules",
      "description": "All organs transition ALIVE↔DEGRADED identically regardless of their constitutional or functional criticality (e.g., Arbiter, Nomos, Senate vs. less critical organs). The MSP Genome References section is empty, meaning no per-organ constraints, SLAs, or recovery policies exist. This uniform handling risks violating the future BoR primacy of constitutional organs by allowing them to degrade without priority recovery or isolation.",
      "grounding": "MSP 'Genome References' section — (none at seed). BoR Article VI (Interpretation Rules) and Article III (Core Identity) are scaffolds offering no guidance on organ criticality.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "high"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP-CADENCE-BASELINES",
      "title": "Absence of operational cadence baselines and anomaly thresholds",
      "description": "The spine-transition log exhibits a regular pulse of simultaneous organ degradations followed by recoveries, but no baseline interval, jitter tolerance, or rate-of-change limit is defined in the MSP. This prevents the system (and operators) from recognizing this pattern as either normal background flapping or a symptom of a larger fault. Cadence baselines are a core operational requirement omitted from the seed MSP.",
      "grounding": "MSP 'Rules' section — (none). The MSP Purpose states it should provide 'precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints,' yet temporal invariants are entirely missing.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority": "medium"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-SCOPE-AND-PROHIBITIONS",
      "title": "Undefined BoR scope and prohibited activities leave operational safety unconstrained",
      "description": "All BoR articles remain as scaffolds, especially Article I (Scope of Enterprise) and Article II (Prohibited Activities). During the observed DEGRADED periods, no MSP rule can prevent actions that might later be prohibited, because the BoR provides no boundary. This operational vacuum means the system may execute unsafe or out-of-enterprise actions during instability without any gate.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article I and Article II — undefined. MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR; with no BoR constraints, the MSP cannot enforce operational safety limits.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "critical"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "MSP-ESCALATION-PROTOCOL",
      "title": "No escalation or notification protocol for persistent degradation",
      "description": "The MSP contains no rules regarding human notification, automatic rollback, or restricted action modes during sustained organ degradation. The spine records repeated DEGRADED episodes with no intervening human interaction events, indicating a lack of operational contact points (HOM notifications) for human oversight during instability. This gap undermines the human authority reservation required by BoR Article IV.",
      "grounding": "MSP Amendment Protocol section describes PEM-based human notification for policy amendments, but does not address operational degradation escalation. BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) scaffold requires eventual enforcement of human-in-the-loop for critical operations.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "high"
    }
  ]
}
